reasoning: The stock price has experienced an explosive rally, surging from around $140 in early September to $218.84 on October 24 (the reference price). This extremely rapid ~56% run-up over six weeks, which included a $12 single-day jump just before the cutoff date, places the stock very close to the upper bounds of recent analyst price target upgrades ($220-$250) and far above the consensus targets from early October (~$185). Such steep, parabolic, short-term moves frequently lead to consolidation, profit-taking, or mean-reversion in the immediate weeks following the peak. Therefore, the probability that the price will be even higher than this local maximum over the very short term (1 week) is slightly below 0.5. Over longer horizons, the underlying positive tailwinds (strong AI-driven HBM demand and expected strong forward earnings) as well as general positive drift in equities will likely counteract the initial overbought conditions, bringing the probability back to or slightly above 0.5. My estimates reflect this gradient, with uncertainty increasing and moving the prediction closer to 0.5 as the forecast horizon extends.
